mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of rising rates are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an impending economic contraction, prompting investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5% over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate broad-based demand expansion across the real economy, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not statistically higher than the market average during concurrent upswings.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the central bank simultaneously raises policy rates, the market prices in a higher probability of an aggressive tightening cycle that overshoots and triggers a recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral during concurrent CPI and rate hikes, or the interaction term in a regression model shows no statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP serves as a direct proxy for aggregate economic activity and corporate profitability, generating a persistent positive trend in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions adjust upward.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between GDP growth surprises and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.20 over a 5-year period, or forward returns are flat during consecutive quarters of GDP expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls indicates weakening aggregate demand and early-stage labor market cooling, leading investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict market declines exceeding 3% over the 20-day window in more than 50% of historical instances, or forward returns show no significant difference between contraction and expansion payroll periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coupled with accelerating consumer prices signals supply-side bottlenecks and cost-push inflation, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite concurrent rises in INDPRO and CPIAUCSL, or the interaction effect on forward returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, rising consumer credit outstanding alongside increasing housing starts reflects sustained household leverage and demand, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days until debt sustainability breaks.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when both series rise simultaneously, or the predictive power of the credit-housing interaction decays to a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
